summaryHtml:
This validation list announces the official recognition of ten new bacterial names and one new genus under the nomenclature procedures of the International Journal of Systematic and Evolutionary Microbiology (IJSEM). The document validates names that were previously published elsewhere but require formal approval through IJSEM for full nomenclatural standing. The validated organisms include Actinomyces radicidentis from infected root canals, several Nitrobacter and Heliobacterium species with various metabolic capabilities, Desulfovibrio dechloracetivorans capable of dechlorination, Nitrospira moscoviensis, Methylophilus leisingeri, and the newly established genus Trichlorobacter with its type species T. thiogenes. Each organism has a designated nomenclatural type strain maintained in recognized culture collections. The validation date is the publication date of this list, not the original publication date. The article emphasizes that validation provides nomenclatural availability but does not constitute taxonomic acceptance, and names may later be recognized as synonyms or organisms may be reclassified.

Key findings

  • Ten new bacterial species and one new genus (Trichlorobacter) were officially validated for nomenclatural use
  • Validated organisms include nitrite-oxidizing bacteria, sulfide-oxidizing heliobacteria, dechlorinating bacteria, and methylotrophic bacteria with diverse metabolic capabilities
  • Each validated name has an assigned nomenclatural type strain maintained in international culture collections (ATCC, DSM, CCUG, etc.)
  • Validation through IJSEM provides nomenclatural availability but does not guarantee permanent taxonomic standing or prevent future reclassification
